{{Quote|I warned you not to fail me!|Darth Vader as he decapitates Jib Kopatha|<ref name="empire25" />}}
'''Jib Kopatha''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] [[Information broker/Legends|information broker]] stationed on [[Void Station]], he was killed by [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] for his failure to provide information.

==Biography==
{{Quote|I've served you well, Lord. I have! It was the smuggler, Solo. I took him prisoner. He was right here!|Jib Kopatha to Darth Vader|<ref name="empire25" />}}
Based out of [[Void Station]], Koptha made a handsome living by dealing in information. Though many of his business practices were illegal, he was able to stay in the good graces [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] by providing valuable intelligence. After not receiving any information from Kopatha for months, [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] lost patience with the [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]]. He went to Void Station, where he violently intimidated Kopatha, warning the Bothan to provide information regarding the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] or face Vader's wrath.<ref name="Star Wars: Empire 19: Target: Vader" />

Kopatha complied and tried to persuade Vader to stay in the city for a few days, offering him various luxuries, including beautiful "company". Among these female servants was a [[Unidentified Naboo female|beautiful young naboo female]], who, unbeknownst to Kopatha, reminded Vader painfully of his [[Padmé Amidala|late wife]]. Vader spurned Kopatha's offers and left the Bothan to contemplate his warning. 

Soon, [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] arrived on Void Station on a mission for the Alliance.<ref name="Star Wars: Empire: Idiot's Array" /> They were betrayed to Kopatha by one of Han's former associates, [[Sheel Odala]]; she owed money to Kopatha. When Odala arrived back at the [[Sabacc/Legends|sabacc]] table, she gave Han a kiss, and then Solo and Chewbacca were surrounded by [[Trandoshan/Legends|Trandoshans]]. She explained to the two smugglers that she had no choice.

Kopatha had Solo [[Torture/Legends|tortured]] by a [[Dug/Legends|Dug]] to extract useful information while Odala watched, though Chewbacca escaped. Kopatha ''generously'' allowed her to leave with her ship and a souvenir, Han's [[DL-44 heavy blaster pistol/Legends|DL-44 heavy blaster pistol]]. At the hangar, Odala met Chewbacca and planned to free Solo. They sneaked through the ventilation and killed the Dug, allowing Han to escape. Kopatha was unable to stop them and called for his guards.

At that moment, Vader arrived once again and confronted the Bothan. He grabbed the Bothan by the throat and told Kopatha he failed to give him information. When Kopatha stuttered that he captured a Rebel, Vader released him, but was not pleased that the Rebel had escaped. Vader swung his lightsaber blade at the Bothan, killing him for his failure. Vader walked away from the body as the lights went off.<ref name="Star Wars: Empire: Idiot's Array" />

[[File:JibKopathaDeath.jpg|thumb|left|Kopatha is killed by Darth Vader.]]

==Personality and traits==
Kopatha was a greedy individual who had no loyalty to any affiliation, only providing information to the Empire to save his own skin. He was a man of wealth and tastes who took pride in the luxuries he could afford.<ref name="Star Wars: Empire 19: Target: Vader" /> Despite his greed, he could be "generous" at times.<ref name="Star Wars: Empire: Idiot's Array" />

==Behind the scenes==
Kopatha was portrayed two different ways between his comic appearances, first with a snout<ref name="Star Wars: Empire 19: Target: Vader" /> and without.<ref name="Star Wars: Empire: Idiot's Array" /> There has been several retcons concerning the appearance of Bothans in the [[Star Wars Legends|Expanded Universe]].
